title: Essential Drawer Bed / Daybed
summary: A low twin-size captain's bed on a 2x4 frame with a full 1x8 headboard and footboard — one or two under-bed drawer pairs on real slides, built into the frame itself.
description:
This is a captain's-bed-style daybed: a low, solid 2x4 frame with a fully-paneled 1x8 headboard and footboard on both ends, built specifically to carry drawers underneath rather than leaving that space as dead storage. Build two identical 2x4 frames first and square each one before the glue sets — checking diagonals now, while everything is still adjustable, is what keeps the drawer openings square later.

The inside cleat gets added early and does double duty: it holds the mattress slats and keeps the 2x4 frame itself square, so it's not just a later-stage part but structural from very early in the build. The headboard and footboard can be built as full 1x8 panels, or — if the bed sits against a wall and full panels feel like overkill — a 1x3 or 1x4 top-and-bottom frame with the middle left open, which the source calls out explicitly as a legitimate cost-saving substitution.

Drawer installation follows the same slide-marking discipline as this catalog's other slide-mounted furniture: mark a horizontal line inset 1" from the front of the vertical 2x4s, center the slide's screws on that line, and use the slide's vertical-adjustment holes so the height can still be tweaked after the first screws are started. This design can carry one drawer pair on a single side (daybed style, against a wall) or two pairs on both sides — doubling the relevant materials and repeating the same drawer-building steps for the second side.

materials.name: 1x8, 6 ft
materials.note: Headboard, footboard, and one side's drawer sides/faces.

materials.name: Drawer slides, 18", full extension
materials.note: One side (two drawers). Double for drawers on both sides.

cutList.part: Center leg and drawer slide furring

cutList.note: Two drawers, one side. Double for both sides.

steps.title: Build the two frames
steps.body: Build two identical 2x4 frames using the longer screws and glue. Adjust each frame for square before the glue sets.

steps.title: Add the inside cleats
steps.body: Secure the inside cleats with glue and screws — this cleat holds the mattress slats later and also keeps each 2x4 frame square.

steps.title: Build the headboard and footboard
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim to the outside edges of the frames with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, then add the 1x8 panels with screws and glue. A 1x3 or 1x4 top-and-bottom frame with an open middle is a valid cost-saving substitution if the bed sits against a wall.

steps.title: Join the frames and add end slats
steps.body: Adjust the whole bed for square until the diagonals match. Secure the end slats with screws and glue, double-checking the diagonals again before the glue dries.

steps.title: Add drawer slide blocking
steps.body: Attach the 2x4 blocking inside the bed for the drawer slides — the center leg does not get attached yet.

steps.title: Mount the drawer slides
steps.body: Draw a horizontal line inset 1" from the front of the vertical 2x4s. Separate each drawer slide into its cabinet and drawer members, and attach the cabinet member with screws centered on the line, using the vertical-adjustment holes so the height stays adjustable.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Mark the drawer sides 4-1/2" up from the bottom and attach the drawer members there, front edge flush with the drawer side's front edge — mirror the second side. Install all drawer sides into the slides, then measure and cut the fronts and backs to fit and attach with the shorter screws.

steps.title: Add drawer bottoms and faces
steps.body: Measure and cut the plywood drawer bottoms, attach with glue and 5/8" brad nails, and test the drawers before the glue dries. Insert the drawers, position the faces with an even 1/8" gap on all sides, tack on with 1-1/4" brad nails, then open each drawer and add six screws per drawer from inside for real hold.

steps.title: Add the remaining slats
steps.body: Lay the remaining slats inside the bed. Attach the center slat with screws, test the drawers to confirm they still slide freely, then screw the center slat to the center leg.

images.alt: Low captain's-bed-style daybed with under-bed drawers
